Registry / Terms / measuring instrument

measuring instrument

VIMOutdated — VIM 1993 G 18 #00258 · 11 instances

Authoritative definition

VIM 1993 · concept #4.1view ↗

device intended to be used to make measurements, alone or in conjunction with supplementary device(s)

Outdated: Cites VIM 1993. Latest: VIM 2012.
  • VIM 1993 · #4.1
    device intended to be used to make measurements, alone or in conjunction with supplementary device(s)

Suggested actions

  1. INFO ✓ This term IS in VIM 2012 (concept #3.1). View VIM 2012 entry
  2. MEDIUM 10 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

10 distinct definitions across 11 publications. 1 definitions are shared by multiple publications (merge candidates).

2 identical2010202X
a device intended to make a measurement, alone or in conjunction with other equipment [VIM, 4.01]

device intended to be used to make measurements, alone or in conjunction with supplementary device(s)

device used for making measurements, alone or in conjunction with one or more supplementary devices (VIM3, 3.1)

device used for making measurements, alone or in conjunction with one or more supplementary devices [VIM 3.1]

202X2022OIML D005:20223.1103277
device used for making measurements, alone or in conjunction with one or more supplementary devices [VIM, 3.1]

202X2023OIML D031:20233.2.3303275
device used for making measurements, alone or in conjunction with one or more supplementary devices adapter from [OIML V 1:2022, 0.10]
pending
202X2012OIML D001:20123.1.402204
In this Document, the term “measuring instrument” covers measuring instruments in the sense of the VIM (see VIM, 3.1), as well as measuring systems (VIM, 3.2), material measures (VIM, 3.6) and any part of a measuring instrument or measuring system which can be the object of specific requirements and of a specific evaluation of conformity. VIM 3.1 – measuring instrument device used for making measurements, alone or in conjunction with one or more supplementary devices
pending
20102004OIML R 136-1:2004T.1.201881
instrument intended to be used to make measurements, alone or in conjunction with supplementary device(s)
pending
